steps.title: Identify the stated goal
steps.content: The task is to emphasize the order in which the two students attended the Bauhaus. According to the notes, Koch-Otte began in 1920 and Stern began in 1930, so Koch-Otte came first. The correct choice must explicitly show that chronological sequence — not just list both students or focus on unrelated details like directors.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Founded in 1919, the Bauhaus was a German art and design school whose past students include Benita Koch-Otte and Grete Stern.': Lists both students but states no order between them; neither is said to come before the other. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Walter Gropius founded the Bauhaus in 1919 and acted as its director until 1928, at which point Hannes Meyer succeeded him in the role.': Focuses entirely on directors; neither student is mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Benita Koch-Otte and Grete Stern were both Bauhaus students, but Koch-Otte, who began attending in 1920, preceded Stern.': Uses the word "preceded" to make the chronological order unmistakable: Koch-Otte before Stern. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The Bauhaus was founded in 1919, and Grete Stern began attending in 1930.': Mentions only Stern; Koch-Otte is omitted entirely, so no order between the two students can be shown. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
